description: Index of all BPA versions and their key artifacts

v8.conclusions: Beta_eff varies with window W (0.064-0.265). BPA degrades at L=2048 (+7.6% PPL). 35-92% KV savings with adaptive control but compute-bound on W7900.

v9.conclusions: Adaptive W(t)/B_far(t) reduces KV traffic 36-92% but CPU-bound decode prevents latency speedup. Quality degrades sharply at L=2048 (+24.7%).

v10.conclusions: Matched-quality at L=512 (PASS, 0.36x KV) but L=1024 fails due to model context limits. KV savings don't translate to latency on compute-bound GPU.

v11.conclusions: 2.3-5.2% latency improvement at tol=1%. Far budget controller shows no meaningful adaptation — machinery is effectively static.

v12.conclusions: Bandwidth-bound at L>=8K. Retrieval predictor weak (AUC=0.626). Scaling exponent beta=1.04 (linear), no sublinear compression without quality collapse.

v13.conclusions: Unified Tiered KV (bitter0-7): bitter1-3 PASS@3% up to 32K with W_sink=4. Scaling beta~1.0 (linear). Frontier methods (MLA/KVSplice) fail from lossy projections.

v14b.conclusions: Only INT8 quantization passes 3% gate. K is highly compressible (rank~19/64) but V is not (rank~63/64). KVSplice gets latency gains but catastrophic PPL loss.

v15.conclusions: rope_complex achieves FULL PASS@1% by compressing RoPE magnitude (position-invariant, low-rank) while preserving phase. All other structural methods fail.

v16.conclusions: Mixed-precision S2 (18xINT4+6xINT8) achieves kv_ratio=0.333 FULL PASS@1%. Sensitivity-guided layer assignment solves cumulative INT4 degradation. No latency benefit on compute-bound W7900.

v18.conclusions: Adam v-hat proxy (rho=0.40) is cheap but inferior to empirical oracle. Cannot match S2 at same budget. Parameter-level curvature misses activation-level sensitivity.

v19.conclusions: 95 signal variants tested; none match oracle S2. C3_residual_ratio is best proxy (rho=0.394). Budget (k-floor) is the bottleneck, not ranking quality.

v20.conclusions: k*=4 is architectural floor for g=32. Tight-group g=4 reduces noise 8x but cannot eliminate k-floor. K noise dominates (not V). Layer 0 accounts for 25% of total noise.

v21.conclusions: g=4 hypothesis disproven by 50% scale overhead. g=32 k=4 with theory ranking achieves kv_ratio=0.3203 PASS@3%, beating S2_k6 by 3.9%.

v22.conclusions: Token-window amortization (g=8, S=8, k=4) achieves kv_ratio=0.3073 PASS@3%. 1.5B replication: residual_ratio proxy fails catastrophically. W7900 still compute-bound.

v23.conclusions: Two sub-0.30 PASS configs: amort_g8_S8_k3 on 0.5B (0.2969) and g32_k2_oracle on 1.5B (0.2974). k/D scaling confirmed: k*=4 at D=24 drops to k*=2 at D=28. 3.26x capacity gain. W7900 compute-bound.

v24.conclusions: O(1) hypothesis SUPPORTED: k*=2 for both D=24 and D=28. k/D decreases 0.083 to 0.071. Sink dominance increases with model scale (tail fraction 33.6% to 0.85%). Accumulation model formalized.
